content:
Is the old computer still available? If so, please try these steps:


  1. ensure thunderbird is NOT running on old machine
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media
  5. ensure thunderbird is installed on second computer, started once and NOT running
  6. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  7. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder. DELETE the thunderbird folder there
  8. now , paste your copied thunderbird folder there. 
#   start thunderbird and all should be there
creator: davidsk

content: When you pause the mouse cursor over the vertical dividing line that your arrow is pointing at, it should change to a horizontal double-sided arrow. Click and hold, and you can drag it to the left. This is for Windows and Linux - I expect Apple works similarly.
creator: rednoise

content:

creator: mozzzz

content:
What I can see of that partially-showing pane looks unusual. It seems to have elements of both Calendar and Tasks arranged together in a way I haven't seen before. However, I have never used Tasks, so maybe it's just that I'm unfamiliar with it. But do you use any tricks/hacks to manipulate the interface, like a custom userChrome.css file?

Some things I would try...

- Maybe it's a misbehaving add-on. Restart Thunderbird in Troubleshoot Mode (<Help | Troubleshoot Mode>). which will temporarily disable all add-ons.

- Try toggling the various Calendar View options found in the <View | Calendar > and <View | Today Pane>, also <Events and Tasks | Calendar> and <Events and Tasks | Tasks>. Maybe exercising those settings will tickle the resize cursor back to life.

That's all I can think of at the moment.
creator: rednoise

content: The relay error often means the sending email address and the SMTP email address do not match.
creator: davidsk

content:
You are correct. I reached out to my host again and they asked me to send a screen shot of the smtp settings and just as I clicked send I noticed the authentication box was empty. I went in and changed it to encrypted and now I can send.

Thank you for your quick response.
creator: business3

content: They don't look aligned here, either. Which illustrates my point: I bet you were composing an HTML-format message using a variable-width font, which, as implied by its name, cannot predictably align, since each letter may be a different width. To have a better chance of things aligning the way you want, use a Fixed-Width font, typically Courier. You can change fonts on the fly with the formatting toolbar in the message compose window, and you can change the default font in <Settings | Composition | HTML Style.>
creator: rednoise

Just select the "Email account". You can add your imap account as a new account. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/manual-account-configuration.

Alternatively, in the "Edit" menu, you can edit an existing account.
